      - [Now Assist for Integration Hub](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/d1126349e3564aed52fba141bf947f1b7efacdc0.md) -- Use the ServiceNow Now Assist AI agents to mimic human-like intelligence by using Large Language Models (LLMs) to perform tasks that range from simple automated responses to complex problem-solving. Utilize the conversational ability of Integration Hub spoke actions.
        - [Available AI agents for Integration Hub](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/d2c19d5b7c5ea569b40be84c4918b21829fb8e7d.md) -- Now Assist AI agents are provided with some of the Integration Hub spokes or plugins that are preconfigured and are callable from interfaces such as, AI Agent Studio and Virtual Agent.
          - [Meeting scheduler AI agent](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/70ecbeeaf4c87f4a1df2ea98e1ce8d5444c6046c.md) -- Now Assist for Integration Hub application provides standalone AI agent that mimics human-like intelligence to perform tasks in your ServiceNow instance. Meeting scheduler AI agent that can be used to organize Zoom or Teams meetings and create regular calendar events.
      - [Integration Hub solutions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/ab9cdfce0a8419884590794a67c95fd3ea6d801f.md) -- Automate typical business processes with Integration Hub solutions. Solutions combine pre-built flows, actions, and other integration-specific content into one package, for quicker, simpler process automation.
        - [Client Software Distribution](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/1f543205dc91c9c69b345baf3307f5606b6a858f.md) -- Use Client Software Distribution (CSD) to distribute software from the Service Catalog using third-party management systems.
          - [Request CSD](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/e2aa7d885f09c70e30bd5a650f55870ed68257c3.md) -- Client software distribution requires the Orchestration - Client Software Distribution plugin (com.snc.orchestration.client_sf_distribution), which is available by request with a subscription to Integration Hub.
          - [Components installed with CSD](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/057b854b3b04ab922e7db7f845ce7a4d4793bb2c.md) -- Several types of components are installed with client software distribution (CSD).
            - [Tables](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/71c7cdfd665034ec14d5a668d0bde5cba60bd5df.md) -- These tables are installed with the Client Software Distribution plugin (com.snc.orchestration.client_sf_distribution).
            - [Plugins](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/435c92c87164050821f9ecf6b531d2c8e66ca40c.md) -- These plugins are installed with the Client Software Distribution plugin, if they are not already active.
            - [Roles](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/2b0a4c57f82de927a1fd6a952840be25f20bc099.md) -- These roles are installed with the Client Software Distribution plugin.
            - [Script includes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/6d5596f7f2ac6de99281ed20eeaa16afdee6d554.md) -- These script includes are installed with the Client Software Distribution plugin.
            - [Properties](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/06b1b4789a8dbe12fca80a7bd771fdb46932fe5e.md) -- Properties for client software distribution (CSD) manage the installation status of requested software deployments and configure scheduled Discoveries.
            - [Flows and subflows](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/f3be20a50422693c5ee0c28a5d5ecc5349daeb6c.md) -- These flows and subflows are installed with the Client Software Distribution plugin.
          - [Create licenses and counters for distributed software](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/cd58e134dc76f0eab70aa2f3ae664413f2fcffb3.md) -- Licenses and software counters are associated with the software model and must be created if you want to track the license for software deployed by client software distribution (CSD).
            - [CSD ordering process](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/cd58e134dc76f0eab70aa2f3ae664413f2fcffb3.md) -- Licenses and software counters are associated with the software model and must be created if you want to track the license for software deployed by client software distribution (CSD).
          - [Extend a software lease](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/f0ab7e3220be3a46232542d26cc9249e391bbc3a.md) -- Users of software deployed by Client Software Distribution (CSD) can request the extension of a lease window, if the software is revocable by a software distribution system.
          - [CSD from SCCM](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/72ceaae1e25880575b2bcc2a57a9901a69fc5bc3.md) -- Use Client Software Distribution (CSD) to deploy and revoke software deployments from Microsoft System Center Configuration Management (SCCM) and manage distributions on SCCM hosts.
            - [Configuring SCCM](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/ef05285d4c58ae9ff93c244ff7d73a1f7af751cd.md) -- Follow the SCCM configuration procedures in the order shown.
              - [Configure the Application Administrator role on the SCCM server](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/2c62a056812c5e58eecf28dda28079b9150ff532.md) -- To deploy software using ServiceNow Client Software Distribution (CSD), ensure that an SCCM administrative user has the correct permissions to deploy software and that PowerShell is properly configured.
              - [Update the SCCM cmdlet libraries](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/1d6a7b42467035f512363d9824c79abcac70630b.md) -- Ensure that the System Center Configuration Manager SCCM Cmdlet Library is up-to-date.
              - [Configure the MID Server for SCCM activities](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/16efc96d9aff4f4e39825db50062e86c694dc002.md) -- To use a MID Server with Microsoft System Center Configuration Management (SCCM) activities, configure it to communicate with the SCCM server.
              - [Create Connection and Credential alias for SCCM deployments](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/68fa11e4b9b0a9e149939dc0a36d067e196c15a0.md) -- Microsoft System Center Configuration Manager (SCCM) requires the appropriate credentials to deploy applications using the Client Software Distribution application.
              - [Retrieve SCCM data for client software distribution](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/1e80f16b853b478a67ac09eb1f487834b04d7ae8.md) -- Retrieve the collections and the list of applications available for deployment from your SCCM servers.
                - [SCCM server Discovery for client software distribution](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/6ad40aefdc2a96be2f1c800bf86a1d53c95a225f.md) -- Discovery flow populates ServiceNow tables with collection, application, and deployment data retrieved from SCCM servers.
              - [Set up a software model for an SCCM application](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/7a9cc922f87d1409e632e5ec8c385122e70afa4b.md) -- Using the applications discovered on the SCCM server, set up a software model used by the Software Asset Management application to manage licenses.
              - [Define an SCCM configuration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/5a286e64ed87aab6f2de39772995e7caed1f2c55.md) -- To create catalog items for SCCM software deployment or to configure your instance to revoke software through SCCM, you must first associate that software with a collection through an SCCM configuration.
              - [Create a catalog item for an SCCM application](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/92089d27de58c3a89526578461f5239161a45ab7.md) -- Create a catalog item for an application you want to offer for distribution from the service catalog using the applications discovered on the SCCM server.
            - [SCCM deployment process](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/c55ad03573376b7672e57c9b6c6ae71b8c58f9f3.md) -- Order an SCCM application from a client software distribution (CSD) catalog item in the service catalog triggers the Order Client Software flow.
            - [CSD validation process](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/f1c4444f378ef99f0e1f5b83f6ffb96e96244044.md) -- After SCCM deploys software to a target computer, client software distribution (CSD) detects the installation and validates its status.
            - [SCCM software revocation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/6539128afbb5386f6436a60466327934b73319cc.md) -- Revoke software without any user interaction using Microsoft System Center Configuration Manager (SCCM) if the software configuration specifies an SCCM uninstall collection, even if the software was installed by some other process or user.
              - [Revoke software deployed through the service catalog](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/a1f83e3fefcd5c159ed97ee60ef1a635fa160cce.md) -- Software deployed by SCCM can be revoked, but only when the status of software is Installed and the application associated with the software configuration has an uninstall collection configured.
              - [Revoke unentitled software](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/94acff87ba72a433aebda450fd4c5e09979eb075.md) -- Revoke software using Microsoft System Center Configuration Manager (SCCM) that a user is not entitled to use even if the software was installed by some other process or user.
              - [SCCM software revocation flow](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/d25bd23c866505a4d8c211f6d851b2518f2c19c7.md) -- The Revoke SCCM Application flow moves a user or device from an install collection to an uninstall collection to revoke software installed from Microsoft System Center Configuration Manager (SCCM).
          - [CSD dashboard](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/b071a1edacc2c84867db8461b57bd37faa7db3ea.md) -- The CSD dashboard provides a collection of visual reports for the Client Software Distribution application.
          - [CSD extension framework](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/63539517ac64d049bcfff10cf38e7b9ba8ffe36c.md) -- Client software distribution (CSD) provides built-in extension points for integrating a ServiceNow instance with client software distribution providers, such as Casper, Altiris, or LANDesk.
            - [Configure CSD providers](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/d034ebf95faf2a2a694cc7c749e7d8feca1a722e.md) -- Identify the provider and specify the flows, subflows. and extension points for a customized software distribution process.
            - [CSD extension keys](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/632392191d913a7b5b3e529f2e9f246cb83f0b7e.md) -- Client software distribution (CSD) extension keys allow you to customize the deployment and revocation of software from distribution providers.
            - [CSD extension points](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/fee274660d37ee3061d3d111e7c3c4d52fe79774.md) -- The client software distribution (CSD) extension points create the customization code for the specific provider and the extension keys.
        - [Client Software Distribution 2.0 application](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/931d6a6a788e3dad60359e6aad1736359bced279.md) -- Client Software Distribution 2.0 application (CSD 2.0) application enables administrators to distribute software from the service catalog using third-party management systems.
          - [Request the CSD 2.0 application](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/f1ca417aa6acd15a5fb79dd6886ab33057ef75ad.md) -- Client Software Distribution 2.0 application plugin (com.sn.csd) is available on ServiceNow Store and can be used with a subscription to Integration Hub.

            - [Tables installed](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/ad418572e24130b374db3f28b58d7d1eb7ad502d.md) -- Several types of components are installed with the CSD 2.0 application.

            - [Properties installed](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/ad418572e24130b374db3f28b58d7d1eb7ad502d.md) -- Several types of components are installed with the CSD 2.0 application.
            - [Flows, subflows, and actions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/ad418572e24130b374db3f28b58d7d1eb7ad502d.md) -- Several types of components are installed with the CSD 2.0 application.
          - [Create licenses for distributed software using CSD 2.0](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/bcedf8319c2fcddaec85eca4eb6e68d35a25d76d.md) -- Licenses are associated with the software model and must be created if you want to track the license for software deployed by CSD 2.0.
          - [Create entitlements for distributed software using SAM](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/ccedcf9f1d972a567c35e26ca9ee2c0457ee89df.md) -- Entitlements are associated with the software model and must be created if you want to track the license for software deployed by CSD 2.0 using SAM.
          - [Client software distribution ordering process](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/72a02fb41e33f67a9a5cb9734fc86fdd39726d73.md) -- Software deployed by CSD 2.0 can be ordered from the service catalog by individual users or by approved users on behalf of others.
          - [Extend a software lease](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/8bfb37becd72e3817ce231dc920d450a196e4ae7.md) -- Users of software deployed by CSD 2.0 can request the extension of a lease window, if the software is revocable by a software distribution system.
          - [CSD 2.0 for Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/4337fdce56f1b0de3008c0b4b1d9130ac55e1222.md) -- Use the CSD 2.0 along with the Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager spoke to deploy and revoke software deployments from Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager and manage distributions on hosts.
            - [Verify the Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager spoke records](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/b2931c2209a227f3401bbe5c41a5d38f067bfefd.md) -- Verify that the spoke is integrated with CSD 2.0 after setting up and configuring the Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager spoke and create server instance record to use Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager spoke with CSD 2.0.
            - [Store details of the provider server](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/d2dec446c3c8369e6972ca2047d7957d4ce07a8f.md) -- Create a server instance record to discover applications and store details of the provider server.
            - [Set up a software model for the Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager application](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/53501d1fe95d3474d5a0edcfb83c77a778530dc8.md) -- Using the applications discovered on the Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager server, set up a software model to manage licenses.
            - [Define the Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager configuration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/e94bc94c3b0954004c9f28c5d679dfa82f6b134c.md) -- Associate that software with a collection through a Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager configuration to create catalog items for Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager software deployment or to configure your instance to revoke software through Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager.
            - [Create a catalog item for the Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager application](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/0fd3fefd609cc35da8ab6441fc1c4f8858002127.md) -- Create a catalog item for an application you want to offer for distribution from the service catalog using the applications discovered on the Microsoft Endpoint Configuration Manager server.
            - [Deployment process](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/f533fe3ca7ea079338bf4f2aaa69529989b8cd02.md) -- Order an application from a CSD 2.0 catalog item in the service catalog triggers the Order Client Software flow.
          - [CSD 2.0 for Jamf](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/ce4decd5296136d57481c1d8ee345d4411c139e9.md) -- Use the CSD 2.0 along with the Jamf spoke to deploy and revoke software deployments from Jamf and manage distributions on hosts.
            - [Verify the Jamf spoke records](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/9b0c30ab01a15b9a3f636c3d5788a606f9763fd4.md) -- Verify that the spoke is integrated with CSD 2.0 after setting up and configuring the Jamf spoke and create server instance record to use Jamf spoke with CSD 2.0.

            - [Set up a software model](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/9ee7c9ac78766794d936a2daa22df094c76b297d.md) -- Using the applications discovered on the Jamf server, set up a software model to manage licenses.
            - [Define the Jamf configuration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/b5d6b19a62b8f4c8d179db1428a5851bd964bd13.md) -- Associate that software with a group through a Jamf configuration to create catalog items for Jamf software deployment or to configure your instance to revoke software through .
            - [Create a catalog item for the Jamf application](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/b7eac5d564a5d2b5904794608a6f600bcf390af4.md) -- Create a catalog item for an application you want to offer for distribution from the service catalog using the applications discovered on the Jamf server.

          - [CSD 2.0 for Microsoft Intune](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/6189827d5d78ab85cfde02f820e9c072b0a43fdd.md) -- Use the CSD 2.0 along with the Microsoft Intune spoke to deploy and revoke software deployments, and manage distributions on hosts.
            - [Configure group assignments](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/4e50e11964a96fbb7df7a273aaec611343292b76.md) -- Configure group assignments for all the applications you want to deploy.

            - [Store details of the Microsoft Intune server](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/5823b666c62ea08ad7892e663cdd1d8a4163fcb0.md) -- Create a server instance record to discover applications and store details of the Microsoft Intune server.
            - [Set up a software model](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/74002c65affefe3806856725af8193c6cc5538db.md) -- Using the applications discovered on the Microsoft Intune server, set up a software model to manage licenses.

            - [Deployment process](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/affa08dfe034281555cd182b3cb0a38e4f4c1d75.md) -- Order an application from a CSD 2.0 catalog item in the service catalog triggers the Order Client Software flow.

            - [Revoke software deployed through the service catalog](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/c1d035e6b646625c1faa8e9bb819589084a6ad8d.md) -- Revoke software without any user interaction if the software can be revoked and has a lease end date using the provider-specific revocation flow.
          - [Integrate CSD 2.0 with other software providers](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/17f46b43a1d0c3caa420d6f734763588e45fd6e5.md) -- Integrate CSD 2.0 with client software distribution providers, such as Casper, Altiris, or LANDesk.
            - [Create provider tables](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/17f46b43a1d0c3caa420d6f734763588e45fd6e5.md) -- Integrate CSD 2.0 with client software distribution providers, such as Casper, Altiris, or LANDesk.
            - [Create subflows](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/17f46b43a1d0c3caa420d6f734763588e45fd6e5.md) -- Integrate CSD 2.0 with client software distribution providers, such as Casper, Altiris, or LANDesk.
            - [Create a provider record](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/17f46b43a1d0c3caa420d6f734763588e45fd6e5.md) -- Integrate CSD 2.0 with client software distribution providers, such as Casper, Altiris, or LANDesk.
            - [Create UI actions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/17f46b43a1d0c3caa420d6f734763588e45fd6e5.md) -- Integrate CSD 2.0 with client software distribution providers, such as Casper, Altiris, or LANDesk.
        - [Retry Handler Framework](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/d9321a79f61ecb1987074f0ffcf7a9574014777d.md) -- Framework that retries a failed HTTP request when the API rate limits are exceeded and the 429 Too Many Requests status code is returned. The retry-after time HTTP header is used to retry the request again after that specified retry-after time. Also reuse this short description in the release notes.
        - [ServiceNow Kafka Consumer](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/bb2c966a97c23461741f1846e2099c4a63a222a9.md) -- Integrates your ServiceNow instance with Kafka Consumer and stores data in the ServiceNow tables.
          - [Configure ServiceNow Kafka Consumer](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/4e2eba67a773c4107bc828d553e9c09ad04083be.md) -- Retrieve events pertaining to the specified topics and store it in the required ServiceNow tables.
      - [Data Stream actions and pagination](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/8d8b13fc574345e0ccc47606d9848ced0472ee58.md) -- Send REST, SOAP, or JDBC requests from Workflow Studio to APIs that return a stream of response data larger than 10 MB, or that return paginated results. Parse stream data into a series of complex object outputs and use the data pills in other actions in a flow.
        - [Design considerations](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/46faac17a428067d13e296ee028a09e9b09109ff.md) -- Follow these considerations when creating a Data Stream action and when adding a Data Stream action to a flow to prevent errors and performance issues.
        - [MID Server support](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/5ba8c58196e71849d29a7ffd5351cf683f592694.md) -- Get data through a ServiceNow MID Server when running a Data Stream action.
        - [Create a Data Stream action](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/0b118289c9dcb49bfecd464e42d19c056c1c59de.md) -- Create a reusable action to process a stream of response data within a flow.
        - [Generate the Parsing phase](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/e8349f341fdf241057b2afc24d2f344024e86267.md) -- Automatically configure the splitter step, script parser step, and outputs for REST-based Data Stream actions. The Test REST step functionality in REST-based Data Stream actions executes a request to the configured REST endpoint, analyzes the response payload, and automatically sets up the parsing and output components.
        - [Test a Data Stream action](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/521e3fc3aba8b293abd85d3a337d183df17ae375.md) -- Test your Data Stream action to ensure that it works the way you expect before you add it to a flow.
        - [Use a Data Stream action in a flow](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/3be650467876ba14a0a9b0ca73c0c1375eb3696c.md) -- Data Stream actions use the For each flow logic option to process stream data in a flow. For example, suppose that you want to use a Data Stream action that imports document data from a third-party site. When added to a flow, the action appears as a For each flow logic block, enabling flow designers to easily create a record in ServiceNow for each object in the data stream.
      - [Conditional and event-driven inbound integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/ece93290bb25ebad9acec13918ec7a76b1dd2114.md) -- Integration Hub enables both conditional and event-driven inbound integration with third-party applications.
