      - [Integration steps](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/5e0d415594d3b8944cd6c642223153d53c9136d1.md) -- Enable custom actions to integrate with external systems by activating Integration Hub, which adds integration steps to the Workflow Studio interface.
        - [JDBC step](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/213f01ff50807094dd30b9af6be6c4c2c0bc384f.md) -- Create a reusable action to send SQL commands to a relational database.
          - [Test](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/066335b614a11c138d937ebad76d21d2e6dcc036.md) -- Test the JDBC step before testing or publishing an action that contains the JDBC step.
        - [JSON Builder step](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/e6b45aa7f797bda3a3e0889e2a017072d939d6a6.md) -- Create a JSON payload to use in another step. Enter values or use data pills to produce a dynamic payload. This step supports several data types, including objects and arrays for nested structures.
        - [JSON parser step](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/25d1d7e2ce6f2e9dc16132877c46945efb68a17e.md) -- Identify structured data from a JSON payload without having to write a script. Map incoming JSON content to a complex object output that you can use in other steps or actions. At runtime, values from a JSON payload populate the complex object output.
        - [Payload Builder step](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/43e85b8514a1d881521be1b55b4894649f6584ab.md) -- Enable action designers to easily create name-value pairs in JSON and XML payloads using dynamic data.
        - [PowerShell step](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/d78e077dd8eba927cbb9fc5c2a1046bf2226527b.md) -- Run PowerShell scripts on remote machines from your ServiceNow instance through a MID Server.
          - [Configure logging](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/ae5fc8608ee3a8f5ff2de472e0cc9bab59027b14.md) -- Add log messages to the scripts in your PowerShell step. Use log levels for improved logging and debugging.
          - [Test a credential](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/c0c5cc279e85776380277139144f9e0a1b0c91d9.md) -- Test a configured credential in the PowerShell step. Confirm the credential is working before building the rest of your step.
        - [REST step](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/b421b7821f0a7e76c93da6e9ed532d7dd0701d43.md) -- Send an outbound REST web service request to an external system.
          - [Import a REST message](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/6314c83744bcb937cd6ab841f89e6c3afc191b4b.md) -- Transfer data from an existing Platform REST message into a REST step.
        - [OpenAPI support in the REST step](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/5fa58ea6e7a757405ce619c6323849f10497c72e.md) -- Populate REST step fields and action inputs with information imported from an OpenAPI Specification. Import specifications by providing a URL to the YAML or JSON, or copying and pasting content.
        - [SOAP step](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/8c6c38ec689865059e4fe37abb8c3950d1b4ab53.md) -- Enable action designers to send outbound SOAP web service requests to external systems.
        - [OpenAPI/Postman step](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/784193e7a06f6df41987bea0a0776fa887665154.md) -- Import the OpenAPI specification or a Postman collection of a third-party outbound REST web service and build an integration to the web service. The request details for the underlying REST API operation are derived from the OpenAPI specification or Postman collection.
        - [SSH step](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/c3b552c4f0060014ee2dd42bb4d0cfb5e7772d4c.md) -- The SSH step executes SSH commands on an external *nix system through a ServiceNow MID Server. The step also stores scripts and commands for the *nix systems.
        - [XML parser step](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/62357331b71fa3eb9655828cf763f734de15277a.md) -- Identify structured data from an XML payload without having to write script. Map incoming XML elements to a complex object output that you can use in other steps or actions. At runtime, values from an XML payload populate the complex object output.
      - [REST API trigger](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/3c4c50fdb170766bc13032c139fb949e5bf3202e.md) -- Start a flow from an inbound API call or webhook from an external system. Configure the trigger start conditions without having to write or maintain custom code.
        - [Create a flow using the REST API trigger](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/3c4c50fdb170766bc13032c139fb949e5bf3202e.md) -- Start a flow from an inbound API call or webhook from an external system. Configure the trigger start conditions without having to write or maintain custom code.
      - [Password reset](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/bb58b7a81f2dac58c78603ef3906891af7301664.md) -- Create subflows in Integration Hub to automate and customize the password reset process for users managed by third-party applications such as Okta or Microsoft Entra ID.
    - [Import and stream data](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/6261ad3836f07a75efd30750c7f87f58307f4609.md) -- Import data with Integration Hub - Import or stream data from your Apache Kafka environment with Stream Connect.
      - [Integration Hub - Import](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/746421ae23156975fb23ff057427e29252b89a8d.md) -- Automate data import tasks through a consolidated interface that provides a guided, step-by-step experience. Define external data sources, specify target tables, map how source data becomes target data, and schedule regular imports.
        - [Configure a data source](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/adb3be23d6a23de3d181d9af9dc7d7df4e3e7dd5.md) -- Start importing data with Integration Hub by creating a data source. A data source specifies how and where to get the data you want to import.
        - [Map source data to a target table](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/034a4020953f69b1e8c60e2d0be66f1dd48666f9.md) -- Specify how source data is transformed to your target tables.
        - [Run or schedule a data import](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/1060a1b3d0a4fe747f5b6d18f9102f518cc74d1a.md) -- Specify when to run a data import by creating a schedule. Schedule an import to run at a specific time, at regular intervals, or after a parent import. You can also run your import immediately.
        - [Transform settings](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/c310eee707ddc913b333c7e765ceb39dee4d4c87.md) -- Configure the transform settings for your Integration Hub - Import integration. Specify batch size and empty-field handling, enable verbose mode, or run a script before or after the integration.
      - [Using Stream Connect for Apache Kafka](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/13bc73599db08968912eea25336f37a506b511b3.md) -- Connect your Apache Kafka environment to your ServiceNow instance with Stream Connect for Apache Kafka.
        - [Quick start guide](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/f7f28841ae7830f0268ef308f76e0d53c9dd7630.md) -- This quick start guide gives you an overview of how to set up and use each of the six Stream Connect producers and consumers to exchange data between your ServiceNow instance and a Kafka environment.
          - [Set up a secure connection to the Hermes Messaging Service](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/f7f28841ae7830f0268ef308f76e0d53c9dd7630.md) -- This quick start guide gives you an overview of how to set up and use each of the six Stream Connect producers and consumers to exchange data between your ServiceNow instance and a Kafka environment.
          - [Test the connection to Hermes through the ADCv2 gateway](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/f7f28841ae7830f0268ef308f76e0d53c9dd7630.md) -- This quick start guide gives you an overview of how to set up and use each of the six Stream Connect producers and consumers to exchange data between your ServiceNow instance and a Kafka environment.
          - [Use the Kafka Message trigger and Script consumer](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/f7f28841ae7830f0268ef308f76e0d53c9dd7630.md) -- This quick start guide gives you an overview of how to set up and use each of the six Stream Connect producers and consumers to exchange data between your ServiceNow instance and a Kafka environment.
          - [Use the ETL and Transform Map consumers to import data](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/f7f28841ae7830f0268ef308f76e0d53c9dd7630.md) -- This quick start guide gives you an overview of how to set up and use each of the six Stream Connect producers and consumers to exchange data between your ServiceNow instance and a Kafka environment.
          - [Use the Kafka Producer step and ProcuderV2 API](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/f7f28841ae7830f0268ef308f76e0d53c9dd7630.md) -- This quick start guide gives you an overview of how to set up and use each of the six Stream Connect producers and consumers to exchange data between your ServiceNow instance and a Kafka environment.
        - [Topic aliases](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/6c6aaabe10d7fe71c1123f06453ab9949c8d95b4.md) -- Use topic aliases to simplify topic management in Stream Connect. A topic alias is a unique topic name that can be connected to any underlying Hermes or Direct Kafka topic. A topic alias can be moved to different instances and connected to a different underlying topic on each instance.
          - [Create and connect a topic alias](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/6c6aaabe10d7fe71c1123f06453ab9949c8d95b4.md) -- Use topic aliases to simplify topic management in Stream Connect. A topic alias is a unique topic name that can be connected to any underlying Hermes or Direct Kafka topic. A topic alias can be moved to different instances and connected to a different underlying topic on each instance.
        - [Message replication](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/13c7daa847820276de1ba5ffb756a00889c542cf.md) -- Replicate data between your Apache Kafka environment and ServiceNow.
          - [Create message and topic replications](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/5611223d7c8e1b7df52d6845739e2efe2de98abc.md) -- Set up message and topic replications to replicate data between your Apache Kafka environment and ServiceNow.
          - [Create Kafka credentials](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/94b5dccb46e5661ad45961df0b34c1bb38a6ee8a.md) -- Configure credentials for your Apache Kafka connection.
          - [Configure a Kafka connection](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/a848eda8fe363dd628c26b91055d4a8f927b3305.md) -- Configure a connection to your Apache Kafka environment.
          - [Viewing message replication statistics](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/3d28bcb1d456c32a3bfbe16b1ce5ed50e6dc9efd.md) -- When message replications are running, the system creates a metrics record for each active topic replication every 60 seconds. Metrics records provide information about topic replications. You can view metrics records on the Message Replication Statistics [sys_sc_channel_replication_metric] table. You can also view metrics records for a topic by checking the Message Replication Statistics on its Kafka Topic Replication record.
        - [Direct Kafka](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/777fce85d3baa0086133f3c75c3778751b079cb6.md) -- Integrate your on-premise ServiceNow instance with your local Kafka environment with Direct Kafka. Build efficient integrations between your enterprise systems, enabling high-volume and robust integrations to exchange data between applications, and reduce data loss with the queuing mechanism if one environment is temporarily offline.
          - [Create a Direct Kafka Cluster](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/777fce85d3baa0086133f3c75c3778751b079cb6.md) -- Integrate your on-premise ServiceNow instance with your local Kafka environment with Direct Kafka. Build efficient integrations between your enterprise systems, enabling high-volume and robust integrations to exchange data between applications, and reduce data loss with the queuing mechanism if one environment is temporarily offline.
        - [Alerting](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/ff36c4f9d072d68b069e9b51c73d33a4331ead91.md) -- Receive alerts and alert notifications for Stream Connect integrations.
          - [Alerting properties](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/21262632e841a335d119660cf6f9279a787f16be.md) -- Specify alert properties and how alerts are managed in Stream Connect.
        - [Using the Stream Connect Dashboard](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/20c263f7df2322706a39c23d96485ebf71f45cad.md) -- View detailed statistics for your Stream Connect integrations. Manage producers and consumers, view data usage, and create or edit topics and replicators with the ServiceNow Stream Connect dashboard.
        - [Schema management](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/27df2d86d056fef2dec3670b4a1a0b5426d4cb28.md) -- Import and create schemas to send and receive messages in an Apache Avro format. Using an Avro format can reduce the size of the payload and simplify your integration to your local Kafka instance.
          - [Import a schema from the Confluent Registry](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/782c5c8a3a0aa18f7ad83f255a8115a949d87310.md) -- Import a Schema from the Confluent Registry to enable your Stream Connect producers and consumers to send and receive Kafka messages in an Apache Avro format.
          - [Create a standalone schema](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/fc45f8b0af57e3c14ece5263891f3de84f6d7934.md) -- Create a schema to enable your Stream Connect producers and consumers to send and receive Kafka messages in an Apache Avro format.
        - [Configure an Extract Transform Load (ETL) consumer](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/ea83103e0382292612dbf223d41d9611dc6d7b1d.md) -- Import and process data from your Kafka environment using your existing Robust Transform Engine (RTE) configurations.
        - [Configure a Transform Map consumer](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/c835933f224e008fea37f1f20d4b39b358bdf7c2.md) -- Import and process data from your Kafka environment using your existing transform map configurations.
        - [Configure a script consumer](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/dba11f65cefeda6626ecf16e8015d81b353ee0b5.md) -- Use a script to import and process data from your Kafka environment.
        - [Create a Kafka stream](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/b5ac0f8d451e382e6ed3b6cfcea1919c02b18a3e.md) -- Define a data stream for an Extract Transform Load (ETL), Transform Map, or Script consumer. A Kafka stream defines the stream of data to your consumer.
        - [Viewing Kafka subscriptions and statistics](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/b15224203db482c2df00bce1915f9e409ad1008f.md) -- View detailed information about a Kafka consumer and its performance, including the number of records added to the topic, the number of records processed, and the number of records remaining to be processed.
        - [Viewing producer statistics](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/ad38d9d07707d921531d0a974da13c600104c155.md) -- View detailed information about a Stream Connect producer and its performance, including the producer type and ID, and the total number of bytes and messages produced to a topic.
        - [MID Hermes API extension](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/99bb9021959f92983a10fa9e391b490c4178e422.md) -- MID Hermes API is a MID Server extension that enables communication to Hermes via a MID Server.
          - [Configure the MID Hermes API Extension](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/99bb9021959f92983a10fa9e391b490c4178e422.md) -- MID Hermes API is a MID Server extension that enables communication to Hermes via a MID Server.
    - [Manage connections](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/b8a4c58270a2290e4b42dba22e46f069c075b4d7.md) -- Create several connections to a single integration provider. View and configure connections in a simplified interface with the Connections dashboard.
      - [Supporting multiple connections](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/39a5f96ddaa4c6dfb475fa8915089cec9c793650.md) -- Support several connections to a single integration provider. Select connections for flows, subflows, and actions directly from a flow.
        - [Override a connection in a flow](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/66b500415f4385c06973fa3a2a64b97a27e3f05f.md) -- Override a connection for a flow, subflow, or action.
        - [Test a connection alias](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/aa0fb2168fb9b55894294ead9bf41c719e57490e.md) -- For connection aliases using a configuration template, you can create a test action that enables you to test the connection directly from the Action Properties section of actions contained in a flow in Workflow Studio.
      - [Using the Personal Authentication dashboard](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/6fb0e2a9989ac7d020e8e689b01158f74d89e264.md) -- Use your personal credentials to connect to third-party integrations. View, authenticate, revoke, and renew your personal authentications through a simplified, consolidated interface.
      - [Using the Connections dashboard](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/271530abe0a7873aa2e3edbd55c3f7698e2c661e.md) -- View and configure your connection and credential aliases through a simplified interface. Add new connections, edit existing connections, and view connection details.
        - [Add a connection](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/ed8b378cbb0dfdc1eafd76edcff9e8e35080124b.md) -- Add a connection through the Connections dashboard.
        - [Configure a connection](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/43f6fab982fad6ab902ce3cd5e5bcadcff79cef0.md) -- Configure a connection through the Connections dashboard.
      - [Using the mTLS protocol with a MID Server](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/f921c87c23d0dac525c27792ea4f483a36167791.md) -- Make outbound REST and SOAP calls through a MID Server using mTLS. Store mTLS credential and certificate information on the instance, in a configuration file, or in an external vault. The MID Server retrieves the credential and certificate information and makes outbound REST and SOAP calls using the mTLS protocol.
        - [Create a MID Protocol Profile](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/f921c87c23d0dac525c27792ea4f483a36167791.md) -- Make outbound REST and SOAP calls through a MID Server using mTLS. Store mTLS credential and certificate information on the instance, in a configuration file, or in an external vault. The MID Server retrieves the credential and certificate information and makes outbound REST and SOAP calls using the mTLS protocol.
    - [Reference](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/fbefdb81740b58c7c0556fd5d83c879b87a93e95.md) -- Reference topics provide additional information about Integration Hub and domain separation.
      - [Domain separation and Integration Hub](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/a2b597d0b469938714f6e9d454affa4194826652.md) -- Domain separation is supported for Integration Hub.
      - [Domain separation and Stream Connect](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/713f5ee819c1988ade67557b8403ed7003c4a5cc.md) -- Domain separation is supported for Stream Connect.
      - [Stream Connect roles](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/a31fcc574bf66f4d31151820135d7a21c2a8a9eb.md) -- Assign roles to specify what different users can see and do.
      - [Direct Kafka usage metrics](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/8d2f64d73b7601448476b3aea1d4678cdfb6ef52.md) -- Direct Kafka Usage metrics table provides administrators with visibility into data usage between your instance and Direct Kafka systems. This read-only table tracks usage metrics on an hourly, daily, and monthly basis. Usage records are automatically retained for 13 months, with a scheduled cleaner job removing records older than this threshold on a monthly basis.
  - [Integration Hub Remote Process Sync](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/f9df41d1c6ce7a612dc792a3ed3f67886535fb87.md) -- Integration Hub Remote Process Sync enables you to set up one-way or bidirectional integrations among multiple ServiceNow instances, so that your process flows stay in sync.
    - [Getting started](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/68373b7671facb13ba3ecf46ccba4822ecaa6994.md) -- Learn the basics of setting up a Remote Process Sync integration in order to link the automated processes among two or more ServiceNow instances together.
      - [Build your first Remote Process Sync integration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/5c6d3688510989fdf58360edd1aa52b805e9fc81.md) -- Step through an example of how to build a Remote Process Sync integration in order to link the automated processes of two ServiceNow instances together.
    - [Remote Process Sync Dashboard](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/154bb59adcd025aaeed6638d0f8069fd5d8927ab.md) -- View detailed statistics for your Remote Process Sync integrations. The Remote Process Sync Dashboard helps you monitor the health and performance of RPS by collecting and visualizing metrics such as records processed, queue sizes, processing times, and error states.
    - [Actions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/38de8a65a5f2d46a64ca0d765aaf59c627405405.md) -- Learn how to use actions in outbound and inbound flows for your Integration Hub Remote Process Sync integration.
      - [Create Correlation action](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/af196e1763d353d8e119d8f9a63f72b87d344f19.md) -- Create a correlation between a record on the local instance and a record on a remote instance. A correlation identifies a remote system and a local record to synchronize. Each instance maintains a copy of the correlation, which contains a set of shared correlation identifiers. Remote Process Sync actions and flows use the correlation identifiers to synchronize records.
      - [Get Attachment Metadata for Local Record action](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/748c729938caa95113a88d69e1aff018b850b5da.md) -- Get the list of attachments associated with a specified local record. Use the output of this action to synchronize the attachments of a local record with the attachments of a remote record.
      - [Identify New Attachments action](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/f1151b5b5013c4036ad477cb8aec41dc2eb6c54d.md) -- Identify the attachments already associated with a local record to determine which remote attachments require synchronization. Use the output of this action to request missing attachments from the remote system.
      - [Look Up Correlation by Local Correlation ID action](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/e279da0994d48dbb2dd99ac695b1472f2d2115dc.md) -- Look up an active correlation by a specified local correlation ID. The local correlation ID identifies the correlation on the local instance. Remote Process Sync actions and flows use the returned correlation to synchronize a local record with a record on a remote system.
      - [Look Up Correlation by Local Record action](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/ecabcf9b2a5f9433c69098d471ac0f57d552a9f9.md) -- Look up an active correlation by a specified local record sys_id. The local record is the record the correlation synchronizes on the local instance. Remote Process Sync actions and flows use the returned correlation to synchronize a local record with a record on a remote system.
      - [Move Attachments action](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/d2216413a6a74ee394363497eb715d1cfcb45a14.md) -- Move attachments from a correlation record to the local record it synchronizes. This action looks up the correlation record by its sys_id.For existing actions and subflows, copy the short description from the product documentation, or insert a content reference to it. For new actions and subflows, create reusable content and insert a content reference to the short description.
      - [Request Attachments from Remote System action](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/5e84b5187a00a0173d89e77af1b300090840a831.md) -- Request the transfer of the identified attachments using the specified correlation. The system moves attachments associated with the record on the remote system to the local system.
      - [Send Captured Payload to Remote System](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/091f359b9ec5b7db0d82c8d442fed9db69028041.md) -- Generate a captured payload, and send it to a remote system. This action should follow a Look Up Correlation action. Remote Process Sync flows use the returned correlation to check if a new correlation should be created.
      - [Transform Payload from Inbound Sample action](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/32aea20783f9de1bbcb1938ab63603c2bf7ce9f3.md) -- Transform a Remote Process Sync payload from a JSON string to a complex object. Generate the object structure from a sample of the inbound payload.
      - [Update Correlation State action](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/e25162b8852eb435e40f0cb090472dd27812efe4.md) -- Enable or disable synchronization of records by the specified correlation. Set an active correlation to inactive to disable synchronization of records. Set an inactive correlation to active to resume synchronization of records.
    - [Sample subflows](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/ebcae5a4c2801ec02a804a0130221795af188631.md) -- Copy and configure the sample subflows to synchronize processes between multiple instances. Use an inbound subflow to parse incoming record data. Use an outbound subflow to send record data to a remote instance.
      - [Inbound Flow Template - Basic](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/3206acac438a3978eae3ab97a4c06a050f4de9d8.md) -- A sample Remote Process Sync subflow to process incoming payloads received from a remote instance. This subflow creates a correlation between a remote record and a local incident record. Copy this subflow and edit the actions that refer to the Incident table.
      - [Outbound Flow Template - Basic](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/102fe43fae351e68515d5b4e669a31b88c7ad289.md) -- A sample Remote Process Sync subflow to send outgoing payloads to a remote instance. This subflow creates a correlation between a remote record and a local incident record. Copy this subflow and edit the input for the local record to the table of your choice.
    - [Correlation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/e1de5d52397e4fdecabfb585f5db25aeec740744.md) -- Establish a synchronization relationship between records that reside on separate instances.
    - [Properties](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/11218a8b4cbc88317a45a1f52bb10e0213777b63.md) -- Refer to the list of system properties for Remote Process Sync to learn how to manage your process-oriented, multi-instance integration.
  - [Robotic Process Automation (RPA) Hub](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/5e05b2423f0fee080b3a73c6a7b9204033c5388b.md) -- Use the ServiceNow Robotic Process Automation (RPA) Hub to enable end-to-end automation for your organization. With a combination of UI interactions and element-based automations that interact between the various business applications, you can emulate user actions and eliminate mundane and repetitive human activities.
    - [RPA Hub](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/d874cfd6d53fe2414b1c5849f99dd3cc9a263362.md) -- ServiceNow Robotic Process Automation Hub (RPA Hub) is a powerful platform for orchestrating and managing your robotic workforce to execute various business processes efficiently.
      - [Explore](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/288b8a669ac653526144ed3617414ada8d60096f.md) -- Learn about the RPA Hub features that enable you to govern, manage, and supervise your digital workforce.
        - [Learn about RPA suite of applications](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/eaf38512573569b203513aca474b21dfaf811865.md) -- Learn about the Robotic Process Automation (RPA) Hub applications, types of automations, benefits, high-level architecture, and workflow.
        - [Architecture](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/11f647b50ecc29d3a5ea95f9d463ebc234dcc6f1.md) -- Learn about the Robotic Process Automation (RPA) Hub architecture.
        - [RPA Hub features](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/d21291d240148904e85676da467ab4284cddbf52.md) -- Learn about the various RPA Hub features and capabilities.
        - [RPA Hub Workspace](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/96332be1e0ecc2e97ad1de7fd751951c8f665f9f.md) -- Learn about the digital workforce that you can manage all from the RPA Hub Workspace.
        - [Internationalization support](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/07aecc274df2c8f10bdac1baea22a377429697e0.md) -- You can customize the RPA Hub application for your international users by using a language plugin with the ServiceNow AI Platform. Internationalization is enabled for only for the RPA Hub instance on ServiceNow AI Platform, but not for thick client applications such as RPA Desktop Design Studio, Unattended Robot, and Attended Robot.
      - [Getting started](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/a19606b2f0effec41ee81fed1e31eefc384bce51.md) -- 
        - [Install Robotic Process Automation (RPA) Hub](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/9201fffa2a7b920611606d6986ae21eae9ebf550.md) -- You can install the Robotic Process Automation (RPA) Hub application (sn_rpa_fdn) if you have the admin role. If the application does NOT include demo data or it does NOT install related applications and plugins, delete or revise the following sentence:The application includes demo data and installs related ServiceNow Store applications and plugins if they are not already installed.
        - [Use Guided Tours](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/352d4766cd0e8e279d8f9c6ffafbd784010c0ae4.md) -- Use Guided Tours in RPA Hub to get instructions on how to perform a task.​ Guided Tours use a series of steps that may span multiple pages. The tour includes links to detailed documentation.​
      - [Configure](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/bd43a83289ad4440438f0fce1d587a4b106f9627.md) -- You can plan and configure an RPA Hub implementation in your organization.
        - [Download the RPA applications](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/ebd99394ac079035a52d3654e417d1ebfb97acfc.md) -- Download the Robotic Process Automation (RPA) applications in your Windows machine from RPA Hub as a prerequisite for installing the applications or upgrading them.
        - [Obtaining client authentication and code signing certificates](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/3e09ed69b5dabb9f35fe04dd0e30f456933bc3ab.md) -- Use mTLS authentication for client authentication and verify authenticity of the automation packages by generating a code signing certificate.
          - [Generating and installing user certificates](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/8447f244208fe2d68591a9ae0ddec1dcfa29c645.md) -- You can generate and install a user certificate for client authentication and code signing in your Windows machine, if you have the admin role.
            - [Generate a user certificate from KeyStore Explorer](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/8447f244208fe2d68591a9ae0ddec1dcfa29c645.md) -- You can generate and install a user certificate for client authentication and code signing in your Windows machine, if you have the admin role.
            - [Install the user-generated certificates](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/8447f244208fe2d68591a9ae0ddec1dcfa29c645.md) -- You can generate and install a user certificate for client authentication and code signing in your Windows machine, if you have the admin role.
            - [Add user to manage private keys](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/8447f244208fe2d68591a9ae0ddec1dcfa29c645.md) -- You can generate and install a user certificate for client authentication and code signing in your Windows machine, if you have the admin role.
          - [Exporting Microsoft Active Directory certificates](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/e82febf6c321bfcd77dcab5d1f2416259f91847f.md) -- You can export Microsoft Active Directory certificates, such as root, intermediate, and a client authentication certificate, for authentication purposes. You then register the exported root and intermediate certificate in the ServiceNow instance and map the exported client authentication certificate to the user.
            - [Export a root certificate](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/e82febf6c321bfcd77dcab5d1f2416259f91847f.md) -- You can export Microsoft Active Directory certificates, such as root, intermediate, and a client authentication certificate, for authentication purposes. You then register the exported root and intermediate certificate in the ServiceNow instance and map the exported client authentication certificate to the user.
            - [Export an intermediate certificate](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/e82febf6c321bfcd77dcab5d1f2416259f91847f.md) -- You can export Microsoft Active Directory certificates, such as root, intermediate, and a client authentication certificate, for authentication purposes. You then register the exported root and intermediate certificate in the ServiceNow instance and map the exported client authentication certificate to the user.
            - [Export a client authentication (active directory) certificate](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/e82febf6c321bfcd77dcab5d1f2416259f91847f.md) -- You can export Microsoft Active Directory certificates, such as root, intermediate, and a client authentication certificate, for authentication purposes. You then register the exported root and intermediate certificate in the ServiceNow instance and map the exported client authentication certificate to the user.
        - [Applying the CSDM guidelines](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/f608db2f8626dbf0de90287a162893adac587b52.md) -- Apply Common Service Data Model (CSDM) guidelines to RPA Hub.
          - [RPA Hub and CSDM tables](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/1beeefeb40dd3e492d2be863234d823fda7d10c2.md) -- RPA Hub manages and uses CSDM tables. Several ServiceNow products benefit from and add value to RPA Hub.
        - [Configure properties](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/da6b7465a6e2be1a1e0bde152773a2b1c67f1734.md) -- You can access system properties for RPA Hub by navigating to All Robotic Process Automation Administration Properties .
        - [Configure the system properties for an activity stream](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/33fb3ca453dc3e2bf6b50e7b9381f587c4687414.md) -- Configure the system properties for an activity stream of work queues, schedules, packages, process parameters, and shared parameters in RPA Hub. You can add or remove the fields that are captured in the work notes of an activity stream.
        - [Display an activity stream](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/feb8bd716845b3235bc56a45d02e503481e3dc27.md) -- Configure the system property to display an activity stream for bot processes and robots in RPA Hub.
        - [Add the ServiceNow RPA Chrome extension](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/24932f929e18b13320d0cca7568e62a9e4a03ba3.md) -- Add the ServiceNow RPA Chrome extension to your Chrome browser to establish an interaction with the applications that are opened in this browser.
        - [Robot license distribution](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/6b4a7d1f1c6cb5b85ef62bcf3af62f75922b9673.md) -- As a user who has the sn_rpa_fdn.rpa_admin role, you can create, write or delete robot license distribution record to define the consumption and distribution of licenses in your organization. You can create only one robot license distribution record per domain in a domain separated instance. Once you create this record, you can edit it, but you can't delete it.
          - [Create a robot license distribution record](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/0a0ff1e90320ee9abdda563daf6e78bb64d67771.md) -- Define the consumption and distribution of licenses by creating a robot license distribution record in RPA Hub.
          - [Update a robot license distribution record](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/137cb4375a8fa6b128e5e83fbcbb0d6e8081de07.md) -- Update a robot license distribution record in RPA Hub to evaluate the consumption and distribution of licenses in your organization.
          - [Re-evaluate license distribution](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/ac6c779fac9c504308c1927407173cde1a2efb36.md) -- Run the Robotic Process Automation (RPA) script on demand to validate and unlock the robots in a domain and its child domains.
      - [Use](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/a1ac5d0d135317c9d475deaf549d6d635b5b252a.md) -- Developers, support users, release managers, and administrators can use the RPA Hub application to start and stop a bot process, launch a bot process in the RPA Desktop Design Studio, execute skills, view the HashCode of a package version, and retire a bot process, package, and a robot.
        - [Configuring a bot process record](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/b8be7c9b923ff9b1d1e13b6ecf3882edef928ea3.md) -- In RPA Hub, you can create a bot process configuration record and assign assets to associate it with a bot process. Or you can just save the bot process configuration record, however it won't be associated to a bot process. It remains orphaned.
          - [Bot process LLCS](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/4a6bc9bcb9ed459a67be65fc2339d8148f17b353.md) -- The State transition of a bot process guides you through the stages of its life cycle.
          - [Execution priority of bot processes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/35672b120735d63678b47b40a1c5eec460b8b5ea.md) -- Learn how to prioritize bot processes efficiently and avoid triggering multiple automations on the same robot. The bot processes are organized by both robots and Windows user credentials.
          - [Create a bot process configuration record](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/259ce2a029ff648934e1538f3206eb9fbeef3c90.md) -- Create a bot process configuration record in RPA Hub so that you can define the preliminary fields for a bot process record.
            - [Bot process configuration form](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/259ce2a029ff648934e1538f3206eb9fbeef3c90.md) -- Create a bot process configuration record in RPA Hub so that you can define the preliminary fields for a bot process record.
          - [Associate a bot process to a bot process configuration record](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/438affd4a0ebf7ae41c86fa558767d8e5bb6f1c9.md) -- Select a bot process configuration record to associate it with a bot process. This action creates a bot process record in RPA Hub.
            - [Bot Process form](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/438affd4a0ebf7ae41c86fa558767d8e5bb6f1c9.md) -- Select a bot process configuration record to associate it with a bot process. This action creates a bot process record in RPA Hub.
          - [Adding details for your bot process](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/712ed37f9db786b7b9da7b6879a352781053b318.md) -- After you create a bot process in RPA Hub, you can add some details for your bot process, such as information about the credential groups, business applications, and more.
            - [Assign business applications](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/d9a5a1678ff5dbd6e42a7a39f4bf7f13b7597429.md) -- Assign a business application to a bot process for an effective event correlation or to get information about assigned business applications. For example, you might want to see information about the SAP application.
            - [Associate credential groups](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/457bec0062fb1d9a6a3cdd5db023818e12e572f2.md) -- Enable a robot to perform an automation in RPA Hub by mapping a credential group to an unattended bot process.
            - [Assign robots](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/41abc0808dd83e7bc7b100ad21c7719a0fc4cd79.md) -- Assign a robot to an unattended bot process to execute the automation (package) that is mapped to it.
            - [Assign process robot credentials](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/c56e78aff4366262d84cfe7ed7feb3c0cf136a86.md) -- Assign a process robot credential within an unattended bot process so that the selected robot can perform the automation in the Windows machine.
            - [Create a schedule](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/abd6be4af10917d69f486c322a22b2b28645b855.md) -- Create a schedule within an unattended bot process to enable users to execute jobs in a planned manner at regular intervals. More than one schedule can be associated with one bot process.
            - [Create a process parameter](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/c02593111e32d024d66b3b37322f94751d430621.md) -- Create a process parameter to store the variables that are used within a bot process. The process parameter that you just created can only be used by this bot process.
              - [Process parameter form](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/c02593111e32d024d66b3b37322f94751d430621.md) -- Create a process parameter to store the variables that are used within a bot process. The process parameter that you just created can only be used by this bot process.
            - [Assign an attended user or group](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/1bffa54aa85873ef8e82cb2b26f68af834a7e921.md) -- Assign an attended user or group to an attended bot process in RPA Hub, so that your user or group can access and execute this automation on Windows machines.
            - [Unassign an attended user or group from an attended bot process](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/02a2f772cb8a329601976b5f59155767e3cc0b89.md) -- Unassign an attended user or a group from an attended bot process if the user is moving out of the business process.
            - [Create a process field parameter](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/85cc9ffb42fc114eeb6b60e6b1ad50be29664e43.md) -- Define process field parameters (dynamic parameters) that are required in the automation (bot process).
            - [Associate a queue](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/e8b2a045bb0e3797ab008059dfa08f781c61fe7a.md) -- Associate a queue to a bot process in RPA Hub so that the bot process can interact with this queue. You can associate a bot process to multiple queues. If the bot process is enabled for robot pool, then only one queue (that is not associated with any other bot process) can be mapped to the current bot process.
            - [View skill package versions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/8a955a0863a7b132919b0db4bf4572f994740d4c.md) -- View a list of skill package versions used in a bot process in RPA Hub to make sure that you’re using the correct version.
            - [Override a skill version](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/0449224027e018e99d4444f160ef8b3ab0db33a9.md) -- Override a skill package version in RPA Hub to use a specific version of a skill package for a bot process. When the bot process executes, it uses the selected version instead of the version that you defined in the automation.
          - [Publish a bot process](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/b53db8ea3410159035ca22efec0f47b15741041e.md) -- Publish a bot process so that you can get it ready to execute a process.
          - [Edit a bot process](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/4d2b5520c9cf1caf772aecb9c4f52623070fc31d.md) -- Edit an existing bot process in RPA Hub to modify a few details.
          - [Retire a bot process](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/dd5aa952aeae213e2e018e47c4d28cd1c1489f8a.md) -- Retire a bot process to turn it off. It's one of the life-cycle stages of a bot process. After you retire a bot process, you can't use it anymore.
        - [Robots](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/c784cb908e502bde84e5714bf40742ebada1910b.md) -- Mimic human actions to perform repetitive, rule-based tasks using robots in RPA Hub. These robots interact with user interfaces and automate tasks.
          - [High density robots](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/3069c0240231516e0ae941a35149bf8bf627e0f5.md) -- Execute multiple automations (bot processes) simultaneously and independently on a single Windows Server machine using high density robots.
          - [Robot LLCS](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/907a53faf7bd9640890cfcbc357aab82a2ff0190.md) -- The state transition of a robot guides you through the stages of its life cycle.
          - [Create an unattended robot](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/c0bebace0e274e946caefbb641d55df461c85ee5.md) -- Create an unattended robot in RPA Hub to run the unattended bot process.
          - [Add assigned studio users to an unattended robot](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/793c1e6effaa8d988c430075fa57d4e8f868243b.md) -- In RPA Hub, assign one or more users (RPA developers or RPA admins) to an unattended robot, so that the users can access the specific robot in the Assigned Bot Process section of the RPA Desktop Design Studio application.
          - [Edit an unattended robot](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/ba76cf98bc35a177746f781830bded8adcf8d8ba.md) -- Edit an existing unattended robot in RPA Hub to modify a few details of the robot, convert the robot to a high density robot, and view the robot state.
          - [Convert unattended robots to high density robots](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/b1304c44ac354dff7e6cd029d8d992fb3f6461ce.md) -- Run multiple unattended automations simultaneously on a single machine by batch converting one or more existing robots to high density robots using the Actions on selected rows option.
          - [Create an attended robot](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/bbbd2384080956021ef13c8e782ae19cdf2bcc1a.md) -- Create an attended robot in RPA Hub to run the attended bot process.
          - [Retire a robot](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/d0a3e2dc4a438488518b230e951dbb6a1067641c.md) -- Retire a robot to turn it off. It is one of the life-cycle stages of a robot. After you retire a robot, it cannot be used further.
        - [Package and package version](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/df3dd8f8a0d93d389f734b321c6dcec724c9bada.md) -- A package is a container that groups together one or more automation logic and their related files, configurations, and dependencies. A package version is a specific release of a package.
          - [Package LLCS](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/04bdfc336b9bcee0bbba7734ae52ae09db8e817b.md) -- The State transition of a package guides you through the stages of its life cycle.
          - [Create a package](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/986f3d8b470dadf9a69f3e541e49ad84be4916ff.md) -- Create an unattended or attended package in RPA Hub to assign it to a bot process to execute an automation.
            - [Package Version form](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/986f3d8b470dadf9a69f3e541e49ad84be4916ff.md) -- Create an unattended or attended package in RPA Hub to assign it to a bot process to execute an automation.
          - [Edit a package](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/5db8f8f8e658e2438683bbc514b3e968c510b20f.md) -- Edit an existing package in RPA Hub to modify the details such as the name or description.
          - [Retire a package](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/6455d5bb96ce31cc38d8e358dc398d15fe538f4f.md) -- Retire a package to turn it off. It is one of the life-cycle stages of a package. After you retire a package, it cannot be used further.
          - [Download a package version](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/8e9859506571167d56b9d7d7a9f9838d419831cd.md) -- Download a package version in RPA Hub so that it can be opened from the RPA Desktop Design Studio to build, debug, or modify the automation.
          - [Override a skill package version for multiple bot processes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/e7bdb0e7581e078e0ceef44a60bf0c0b0f450637.md) -- Override a skill package version in RPA Hub for multiple bot processes that are associated with the same attended or unattended package. You can select the latest version of the skill package without publishing the attended or unattended package again from RPA Desktop Design Studio.
          - [Verify the HashCode of a package version](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/c3884bfbb74018ffb6ba82497d8de198e7f292fe.md) -- Verify the HashCode of a package version in RPA Hub so that you can validate the HashCode of the manually uploaded automation zip file with the associated package version record.
          - [Retire a package version](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/651ef039e73738acd465151248cb27c53e0f5892.md) -- Retire a package version in RPA Hub so that the version can't be used again. After a package version is retired, it can’t be used again.
        - [Credential Management](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/fbd6c67e0f05cad0e4c45b3085c59c4a1c531bdf.md) -- Streamline the credentials that robots use to perform the automation that you defined in the bot process. Instead of creating the same set of credentials for each bot process, you can create a credential group that includes a robot credential, application credentials, and external credentials. You can then associate the credential group to multiple bot processes.
          - [Create a robot credential](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/d1f0027e08a748746b19c0f7e9c7094da3d5bc69.md) -- Create a robot credential so that the unattended robot can log in to a Windows machine and perform the automation.
          - [Smart Card](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/ab406030d2029b4fbf6acdb429be58006cee596f.md) -- Smart card login for a Windows machine is a secure method of logging into the system using a physical smart card instead of a username and password. It’s commonly used in organizations to enhance security, particularly in environments with high security requirements.
            - [Configure a robot credential in RPA Hub for smart card](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/dd953591660b4d08bc524b1ce4f7a55e4a2e0a96.md) -- Create a robot credential so that the unattended robot can log in to a Windows machine that uses smart card for login and perform the automation.
          - [TOTP authentication](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/a3ad59ee15435966e9974467c4d552f2271454b9.md) -- You can configure the Time-based One-time Password (TOTP) fields in RPA Hub that enable the unattended robots to seamlessly authenticate against multi-factor authentication (MFA)-enabled applications.
            - [Create a TOTP authenticator in RPA Hub](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/7dce9c54fbff28332bc77ddf4ee73beaa8786ba9.md) -- Map a multi-factor authentication (MFA) authenticator record with a seed in a robot credential. This mapping results in creating a Time-based One-time Password (TOTP) authenticator record in RPA Hub.
            - [Delete a TOTP authenticator](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/083775e64aca397c1406b0781c16cdee3f380678.md) -- Delete a Time-based One-time Password (TOTP) authenticator record in RPA Hub so that you can dissociate it from a bot process.
          - [Create an application credential](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/d311e3674be9a4b121c0a535007b4c38783ac2d2.md) -- Create an application credential to add the application user name and password. An unattended robot can use this application credential to log in to an application when the automation is executed.
          - [Create a credential group](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/84b017c00afffd14c5175bb427b707c256d1e66f.md) -- Create a credential group to include a robot credential and application credentials that you can reuse in multiple bot processes. Unattended robots leverage the credential group to perform an automation that you defined in the associated bot process.
            - [Associate an application credential](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/84b017c00afffd14c5175bb427b707c256d1e66f.md) -- Create a credential group to include a robot credential and application credentials that you can reuse in multiple bot processes. Unattended robots leverage the credential group to perform an automation that you defined in the associated bot process.
            - [Associate a bot process](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/84b017c00afffd14c5175bb427b707c256d1e66f.md) -- Create a credential group to include a robot credential and application credentials that you can reuse in multiple bot processes. Unattended robots leverage the credential group to perform an automation that you defined in the associated bot process.
            - [Provide users access](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/84b017c00afffd14c5175bb427b707c256d1e66f.md) -- Create a credential group to include a robot credential and application credentials that you can reuse in multiple bot processes. Unattended robots leverage the credential group to perform an automation that you defined in the associated bot process.
          - [External credential vault](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/7849dfb56a45989d7a33d10dda6d221b1e0382d1.md) -- With the external credential vault feature, you can retrieve robot credentials, application credentials, or Time-based One-time Password (TOTP) seed.
            - [Steps to configure an external credential vault in RPA Hub](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/5ece33234f35b7028dff44374b7e73fbc857df2f.md) -- Use this list of steps to guide you through all the tasks of configuring an external credential vault in RPA Hub.
            - [Create an external credential vault record](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/6d3f042e39dec310a3c407d062fc664686b5b391.md) -- Create an external credential vault record in RPA Hub to register your external credential vault for further usage by the robot.
          - [Create an MFA authenticator](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/d3e080bcbff223f3470a001d8153ff7c3cbf1064.md) -- Define the authenticator and token length that acts as an enabler for the Time-based One-time Password (TOTP) authentication in RPA Hub so that the unattended robots can seamlessly authenticate against multi-factor authentication (MFA)-enabled applications.
          - [Retire an MFA authenticator](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/0e8ab0bfc8f7321551f9df5b0b7d5ea4afe190a8.md) -- Retire a multi-factor authentication (MFA) authenticator in RPA Hub so that you can turn off the authentication. After you retire an MFA authenticator, it can’t be used again.
        - [Shared parameters](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/019f1ae00586cae3b8e04b03de5857e449bc996f.md) -- Shared parameters enable you to store the global variables or configurable items in one place. These variables are used across bot processes.
          - [Create a shared parameter](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/a61bc3c7e5d770bddb08d418ac4f7f2d26d8baf2.md) -- Create a shared parameter so that you can store the global variables or configurable items in one place. These variables are used across bot processes.
          - [Assign a bot process to a shared parameter](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/dd9accdd2953c337d3aab6518980fff69c6ac5aa.md) -- Assign a bot process to a shared parameter in RPA Hub so that it can interact with this shared parameter. You can assign more than one bot process to a shared parameter.
          - [Unassign a bot process from a shared parameter](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/0ffbadb1d971fff42c93048e1430ae0022f6409c.md) -- Unassign a bot process from a shared parameter in RPA Hub to stop an interaction with this shared parameter.
        - [Queue implementation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/14eb3ab2aea6528a878ad3c5cb04cf36bfa15c2a.md) -- In RPA Hub, the queues are implemented to distribute the workload among various robots. Work Items (WI) within a queue are processed simultaneously by two or more robots.
          - [Create a queue](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/d6bb8ecca9894eb0bb484691ab775a8cb3865ffc.md) -- Create a queue in RPA Hub to manage the work items that you want the robot to process.
            - [Queue form](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/8abe6854e26484d04d899cd816c8365c673e7c83.md) -- Use the Work Queue form to create a queue. A queue lists the work items that you want the robot to process.
          - [Assign a bot process to a queue](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/9945369af6a92cc5a33e22abcf945514161e32ad.md) -- Assign a bot process to a queue in RPA Hub so that it can interact with this queue. You can assign more than one bot process to a queue. Any robot that is associated with the assigned bot process can insert the work item, pick the work item, and update the work item.
          - [Unassign a bot process from a queue](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/7e96749e26cc61b615455600f7ee788e79b9e71f.md) -- Unassign a bot process from a queue in RPA Hub to stop an interaction with this queue.
        - [Embedded Task Automation (ETA)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/fd282d7386ed082443630659fe9595ce0ffd437f.md) -- With the embedded task automation feature, trigger attended bot processes (attended automations) from the ServiceNow forms, apart from initiating them from the Attended Robot application.
          - [Configuring embedded task automation (ETA)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/36dc06c8a1f3471e7b2c32aaf891e926cbe316be.md) -- Use this list of steps to guide you through all the tasks for configuring an embedded task automation.
          - [Runtime Phase of ETA](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/cf17af3ccc531f0584be021f2ae001a6b1855d4a.md) -- Learn more about the triggering of an attended automation from a ServiceNow form.
          - [Create an attended configuration record](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/533a947db1d3d906854289a2a4f1016f4ffe32dc.md) -- Create an attended configuration record in RPA Hub to trigger an attended bot process that is enabled with embedded task automation.
            - [Map a table field to a bot process field parameter](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/f835025a663d047863e036458adaa3326fc7dd42.md) -- Map form fields of the associated attended configuration table (Table field) to the process field parameters used in the automations. For example, if "First name" is the process field parameter used in the automation, then it’s mapped to the form's 'requested_for.first_name' field.
          - [Activate an attended configuration record](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/50d0c18e4fdf8bdb8d031795e6493720bf5cc62d.md) -- Activate an attended configuration record to enable the UI action that triggers the attended automation on the associated ServiceNow form.
          - [Deactivate an attended configuration record](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/fee6b68ed861e1707a6fb4aea76e89be75a3ed80.md) -- Deactivate an attended configuration to move the record to inactive state.
          - [Edit an attended configuration record](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/1699561a1363f32fad583293f2092edb2f129e0c.md) -- Edit an existing attended configuration record in RPA Hub to modify a few details of the record.
          - [Delete an attended configuration record](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/807ed0f8a9d96ba11cfd1ce538241df573ca4d37.md) -- Delete an attended configuration record that you no longer need.
          - [Attended Configuration form](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/04bc166419833d69d0ef1353d8c1d46d51552381.md) -- Use the Attended Configurations form to associate the table form, from where the automation must be initiated, associate the respective attended bot process, and other related conditions.
          - [Invoke Embedded Task Automation via API](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/fc32c52fefd46544b1b7c780c2ba7beb9f9ffca5.md) -- Invoke an attended bot process (attended automation) from the UI experiences by creating an action.
          - [Conditions for the visibility of UI action](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/b43b7f5e3f711a17a14ff63be8e91c5ce70c8bef.md) -- Ensure the following conditions are met to view the UI action (button) on the corresponding ServiceNow form, for the associated table that is selected in the Attended Configuration record.
        - [Robot pool](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/e71b2c53322dba7a2a82f4f6cec2aa88659279ac.md) -- Create a robot pool in RPA Hub to distribute and manage the workload among multiple unattended robots for unattended bot processes.
          - [Create a robot pool](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/11b7308f7b598aff32dd9b1fd10ae0f7d9c93df8.md) -- Create a robot pool in RPA Hub for an optimum utilization of robots to execute the bot processes.
          - [Enable robot pool for a bot process](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/4f10524d5184f929e7ad7a3bf6853906e86480a2.md) -- Enable robot pool for a bot process in RPA Hub to effectively allocate robots for it's execution and for distribution of work load.
          - [Calculate the number of robots required](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/137a31b88ed81fbf3a998ad332a68350299797b6.md) -- Calculate the number of robots that you need to complete an unattended bot process execution that is based on the allocation type, for example, the SLA Based and Percentage Reduction.
        - [Alert rule and variable](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ServiceNow/ServiceNowDocs/australia/markdown/governance-risk-compliance/c2dcba6cc1c19215eb1c274e7cd5e268409021e5.md) -- A rule that defines when and how the system should notify users about issues in automation execution.
